title: ProcessesJson
description: Per-VM process DAG and lifecycle invariants from ADR 0004.

schemaVersion.description: Schema version used by this artifact.

BindMount.description: Cross-domain bind mount declaration for a minijail role.

dst.description: Destination path inside the role's mount namespace.

src.description: Source path on the host (broker opens on the caller's behalf).

CgroupPlacement.description: Cgroup placement for a jailed role.

delegated.description: Whether the broker must delegate ownership to nixlingd.

subtree.description: cgroup-v2 subtree requested for this role.

DagEdge.description: Directed dependency edge in the per-VM DAG.

MountPolicy.description: Mount policy for a minijail role.

bindMounts.description: v1.1.1 live-deploy fu9: cross-domain bind mounts the broker performs into the role's mount namespace (e.g. a Wayland socket for the GPU sidecar). Each entry is a `{src, dst}` pair. Optional for backward-compat with older bundles.

deviceBinds.description: v1.1.1 live-deploy fu9: closed-set device bind paths the broker opens on behalf of the role (e.g. `/dev/kvm`, `/dev/dri/renderD128`, `/dev/nvidia*`). Optional for backward-compat with older bundles; empty for roles that don't need device access.

hideDeviceNodesByDefault.description: Whether device nodes are hidden unless broker-opened fds are passed.

readOnlyPaths.description: Read-only paths mounted into the jail.

writablePaths.description: Writable paths explicitly documented for this role.

NamespaceSet.description: Namespace flags for a minijail profile.

ipc.description: Whether the IPC namespace is isolated.

mount.description: Whether the mount namespace is isolated.

net.description: Whether the network namespace is isolated.

pid.description: Whether the PID namespace is isolated.

user.description: Whether a user namespace is used.

uts.description: Whether the UTS namespace is isolated.

ProcessNode.description: Single process role in a VM DAG.

argv.description: Full argv for daemon-spawned roles; `argv[0]` is the process title.

binaryPath.description: Absolute execve path for daemon-spawned roles.

env.description: v1.1.1fu11 (Option B): environment variables for the spawned runner in `KEY=VALUE` form. Used to thread session-resource paths (`PIPEWIRE_RUNTIME_DIR`, `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR`, `WAYLAND_DISPLAY`) for audio/gpu/ video sidecars whose libpipewire / libwayland clients can't auto-discover them under the ephemeral runner UID.

id.description: Stable node id used by edges.

planOps.description:
v1.2 broker-executed plan operations that run before the runner spawns. The daemon sends only the opaque `vm_id`; the broker resolves these ops from the trusted bundle.

Backward-compatible: omitted from JSON when empty (serde default).

profile.description: Typed minijail metadata for this role.

readiness.description: Readiness predicates that mark the role available.

role.description: Role kind used by orchestration and minijail profile selection.

unit.description: Optional systemd unit backing this node.

ProcessRole.description: Known role types in the ADR 0004 process graph.

oneOf.description: Optional GPU/graphics sidecar — render-node-only mode (fd-passing, no device bind-mounts). Selected when `graphics.renderNodeOnly = true`; uses broker-pre-NS pattern (ADR 0021).
QEMU media VM runner emitted by local runtime-provider configurations.

Guest SSH readiness probe.

Retained for the SSH-compatibility window (old-generation VMs that predate guestd). New generations use [`ProcessRole::GuestControlHealth`] for framework readiness instead.

Authenticated guest-control Health readiness probe.

Replaces [`ProcessRole::GuestSshReadiness`] as the framework readiness gate on guest-control-capable VMs: readiness is a full authenticated Hello + token challenge-response + Health over the guest-control vsock, not a raw TCP-22 probe. It fails CLOSED.

Host-jailed Wayland filter proxy. Runs as `nixling-<vm>-wlproxy` with the real host compositor socket bound read/write at a fixed in-jail upstream path and the per-VM filter socket at `/run/nixling-wlproxy/<vm>`. Empty host capabilities; mandatory `seccompPolicyRef`; no PipeWire/Pulse socket access.
Cloud Hypervisor API socket info is available.
Guest or sidecar sent a vsock readiness notification.
A Unix stream socket is present and listening, checked non-destructively.
Component-specific predicate named by the emitter.

Authenticated guest-control Health probe. Readiness requires a full Hello + token challenge-response + Health over the guest-control vsock — the host-side probe. Unlike [`Self::ComponentSpecific`] (which reports ready unconditionally and would fail OPEN), this predicate fails CLOSED: it is ready only when the daemon completes the authenticated probe and the guest reports a healthy/degraded state, and never ready for an old-generation / unreachable / auth-failed / timed-out guest. The daemon resolves the per-VM vsock socket, peer credentials, and broker-backed signer from its own trusted state.

RoleProfile.description: Role-level minijail metadata without kernel-version syscall allowlists.

adr_carve_out.description: ADR or plan carve-out reference for uid/root-capable exceptions.

caps.description: Linux capabilities retained by the role.

gid.description: Numeric gid used by the role.

seccompPolicyRef.description: Seccomp policy reference only; syscall allowlists are owned elsewhere.

uid.description: Numeric uid used by the role.

umask.description: v1.1.2fu36: file-creation mask installed in the spawned child before execve. See `MinijailProfile::umask` for the rationale. Roles that bind shared Unix sockets (vhost-user-sound, crosvm-gpu, crosvm video, swtpm) declare `0o007` so downstream consumers (cloud-hypervisor) can connect via the per-VM-runtime default ACL.

userNamespace.description: v1.1.1fu14 (ADR 0021): when `Some`, the broker pre-establishes a per-runner user namespace and writes uid_map/gid_map. The child runs fake-root inside the NS; host-side `caps` should be empty. Set by virtiofsd (v1.1.2 ADR 0021) and swtpm (v1.2) roles. Older bundles omit this field; deserialize defaults to None.

RoleUserNamespace.description: v1.1.1fu14 — single-entry uid_map/gid_map declaration for the per-role user namespace. See ADR 0021.

SpawnRunnerPlanOp.description:
v1.2 additive plan operation emitted by `processes.json` for runner nodes that require broker-side pre-spawn work.

Tag-dispatched via `#[serde(tag = "kind")]` so the manifest JSON uses `{ "kind": "diskInit", ... }` shape (camelCase per bundle schema conventions).

Backward-compatible per invariant I4: the field is absent from JSON when empty (`skip_serializing_if = "Vec::is_empty"` on `ProcessNode::plan_ops`), so older bundles always deserialize cleanly.

oneOf.description:
Create and pre-allocate a disk image file if absent.

Used for the per-VM writable store overlay disk (`store-overlay.img`). The broker validates `target_path` is under `/var/lib/nixling/vms/`, creates the file with `O_CREAT|O_EXCL`, pre-allocates `size_bytes` via `fallocate`, and sets mode + ownership.

ifAbsent.description: When `true`, skip creation if the file already exists (idempotent re-run).

mode.description: Unix permission bits in octal (e.g. `0o600` = 384 decimal).

ownerUid.description: Owner UID — typically the per-VM runner UID.

targetPath.description: Absolute host path for the new disk image.

invariants.description: v0.4.0 invariants that must hold for this VM.

nodes.description: Ordered role nodes in the DAG.

vm.description: VM name from the public manifest.

VmProcessInvariants.description: v0.4.0 invariants preserved in the process contract.

perVmAuditPipeline.description: Every VM participates in the audit pipeline.

tpmOwnershipMigrationWithoutRunningVmMut.description: TPM ownership migration avoids touching running VMs.

usbipGating.description: USBIP is gated by host and VM opt-ins plus env scoping.

WritablePath.description: Writable path declaration with a purpose string.

path.description: Absolute path made writable in the jail.
